product.name: ArShirt Static Pro
product.categorySourceGroup: High Visible Warp Knit Fabrics for Safety Jackets
product.titleDescriptor: 97% Cotton 3% Carbon Antisatic, Vat Dyed Pique Fabric
product.specLine: 220 g/m², 97% Cotton 3% Carbon, Antistatic,Pique, Vat Dyed Knit Fabric
descriptions: 97% cotton 3% carbon, antistatic ESD pique workwear knit fabric with very high chlorinated bleaching and light fastness.
ArShirt Static Pro fabric are produced from the best quality combed yarn Turkish Cotton.
It is resistant to very high sun level, with fastness level of 6 to 7.
ArShirt Static Pro fabrics can be bleached with peroxide and hypochloride without whitening in color.
Suitable for antistatic ESD applications, certified for EN ISO 1149.
Vat dyed ArShirt Static Pro products can be used in the most hard working conditions and in military and workwear area.
97% Cotton 3% Carbon Antisatic, Vat Dyed Pique Fabric
220 g/m², 97% Cotton 3% Carbon, Antistatic,Pique, Vat Dyed Knit Fabric
technicalParameters: Dimensional Change | -5% < Length < +5% ; -5% < Width < +5% | EN ISO 5077
Dry Cleaning Fastness | 4-5 | EN ISO 105 D01
Hot Press Fastness | 4-5 | EN ISO 105 X11
Perspiration Light Fast. | 4-5 | EN ISO 105 B07
Azo Test | No Azo Colorants. | EN ISO 14362-1

possibleButNotAccepted.reason: Application image label 'Antisatic T-Shirt for Gas Stations' suggests a fuel/energy-adjacent use, but this is not the explicit electrical/energy sector defined in the taxonomy (electric-arc, high-voltage, EMI shielding), so it is insufficient for a direct match.

The product is explicitly categorized under the hi-vis warp knit fabric category intended for safety jackets, and lists 'hi-vis' as a core property, directly supporting a high-visibility apparel application.
The product description explicitly states usability in the military area, providing direct though brief evidence for military application.
Antistatic cotton/carbon blend could theoretically be used in metalworking environments, but no explicit welding, foundry, or metallurgy use is stated.

evidence: Antistatic ESD pique workwear knit fabric
Suitable for antistatic ESD applications, certified for EN ISO 1149
primary.rationale: The fabric is explicitly described as an antistatic ESD workwear shirt fabric with ESD/EN 1149 certification, intended for general industrial protective clothing. No narrower named sector (energy, welding, firefighting) is explicitly and directly supported beyond generic antistatic properties, so it falls under general workwear-industrial per rule 15.

evidence: Vat dyed ArShirt Static Pro products can be used in the most hard working conditions and in military and workwear area.
secondary.rationale: The description explicitly states usability in the military area, providing direct though brief textual evidence for a secondary military application alongside the dominant general workwear use.

alternativesConsidered.reasonNotSelected: Application image label 'Antisatic T-Shirt for Gas Stations' hints at energy-adjacent use, but taxonomy requires explicit electric-arc, high-voltage or EMI shielding context; antistatic property alone is explicitly excluded (rule 8).

Category source group mentions hi-vis warp knit for safety jackets, but no fluorescent color, EN ISO 20471 certification, or explicit high-visibility warning use is stated in the product descriptions or specs, only antistatic ESD certification.
No explicit welding, foundry, or molten-metal splash use stated; only generic antistatic cotton/carbon composition.
